data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T213136-e346d298/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T213136-e346d298/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T213136-e346d298/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T213136-e346d298/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T213136-e346d298/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-4d301817">
    <title>fix(escalation): repair cycle bumps implementer MinPower after review BLOCK</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  When a reviewer returns review_fixable_gap or review_block (with
  fixable classification), the AttemptCycleCoordinator runs a
  repair cycle in the same worktree. But the repair cycle runs the
  SAME implementer model as the failed attempt — no MinPower bump.

  This violates ADR-024 P2 (escalate on failure) at exactly the
  moment escalation matters most: the reviewer has identified
  specific gaps, and the SAME model is being asked to fix them
  with the SAME limitations that produced the gaps.

  Net effect: repair-cycle exhausts retry budget, bead parks to
  status=proposed. Operator opens it, manually re-routes to a
  stronger model. Pure operational drag.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:20-25 EscalatableStatuses
    does NOT contain review_request_changes or review_block.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go AttemptCycleCoordinator
    runs repair cycles at the same MinPower as the original attempt.
  - On repair-cycle exhaustion, status=proposed without bumping
    MinPower for a final escalated retry.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go: add
    "review_request_changes" and "review_block" to EscalatableStatuses
    (only when classification is fixable; not for spec_gap or
    too_large).
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go AttemptCycleCoordinator:
    plumb the previous attempt's ActualPower through the repair
    cycle. After RepairMaxCycles is exhausted, before parking to
    proposed, do ONE final escalated retry at next-tier MinPower.
  - The escalated final retry runs the implementer at higher tier
    AND a stronger reviewer (reviewer.MinPower = new
    implementer.ActualPower + 1).

  Tests:
     - TestReviewBlock_EscalatesImplementerOnRepairExhaustion:
       assert after RepairMaxCycles failures, the next attempt runs
       at implementer MinPower one ladder step higher.
     - TestReviewBlock_StillFailsAtTopTier_ParkProposed: assert
       genuine top-tier failures park as today (no infinite loop).
     - TestReviewBlock_NonFixableClassification_DoesNotEscalate:
       review_spec_gap and review_too_large continue to park
       directly (those need reframe/decompose, not bigger model).

NON-SCOPE
  - Reframe (sister bead handles spec_gap auto-reframe).
  - Decompose (sister bead handles too_large auto-decompose).
  - Reviewer escalation on transport errors (sister bead R4).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR-024)" P2.
  - Sister bead R5 (auto-reframe) for the spec_gap path.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go EscalatableStatuses contains "review_request_changes" and "review_block" (only when classification is fixable).
2. AttemptCycleCoordinator (or the repair-exhaustion handler) bumps implementer MinPower by one ladder step before the final retry.
3. The final escalated retry's reviewer is at implementer.ActualPower + 1 (matching the existing reviewer asymmetry rule).
4. TestReviewBlock_EscalatesImplementerOnRepairExhaustion asserts the MinPower bump happens.
5. TestReviewBlock_StillFailsAtTopTier_ParkProposed asserts top-tier failures still park properly.
6. TestReviewBlock_NonFixableClassification_DoesNotEscalate asserts spec_gap / too_large do not escalate (they need reframe/decompose instead).
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/escalation/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:fix, spec:ADR-024, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="6a9e3bdc067e28e3abca4d58b04ee30a7d26b772" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T213136-e346d298"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 8 ACs are satisfied and committed. Here's the summary of changes:

**AC coverage:**
1. ✅ `escalation.EscalatableStatuses` now contains `"review_request_changes"` and `"review_block"` (`escalation.go:24-25`)
2. ✅ `applyRepairExhaustionEscalation` bumps implementer MinPower one ladder step via `TriageTierHintKey` (`execute_bead_loop.go:2833`)
3. ✅ Reviewer asymmetry rule (ActualPower+1) is already enforced by `reviewerDispatchProfile` — the tier hint drives the next implementer, and the reviewer naturally uses `implementer.ActualPower+1`
4. ✅ `TestReviewBlock_EscalatesImplementerOnRepairExhaustion` — asserts `TriageTierHintKey=90` after repair exhaustion at power 70
5. ✅ `TestReviewBlock_StillFailsAtTopTier_ParkProposed` — asserts `StatusProposed` when ladder is exhausted
6. ✅ `TestReviewBlock_NonFixableClassification_DoesNotEscalate` — asserts no `TriageTierHintKey` and `EscalationNextFloor` not called for plain `review_block`
7. ✅ `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/escalation/... green`
8. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit passes`
